title: FLA Destroys Africa Corps Camp with FPV Drones in Kidal Region
detail: The Front de Libération de l'Azawad (FLA) conducted an FPV kamikaze drone strike that destroyed a Russian Africa Corps camp in the Kidal region of northern Mali. The attack, reported by Charter97 and corroborated by TRAC terrorism tracking, targeted a camp occupied by Russian personnel in the Amachach/Tessalit area and is consistent with the FLA's sustained drone campaign that began in early 2026. The FLA also previously struck a TB2 drone control center in Tessalit. This represents the continuation of the Tuareg separatist coalition's asymmetric campaign against the FAMa/Africa Corps presence in the Kidal theater, following the fall of Kidal to the FAMa-Africa Corps column in November 2023. The FLA has used commercially available FPV quadcopters and loitering munitions to target hard-to-access Africa Corps positions across the Tessalit-Kidal corridor, adapting tactics similar to those used in other modern conflicts.

sources.name: Charter97 — Mali rebels destroy Russian mercenary camp in drone attack, April 1, 2026
TRAC Terrorism — FLA FPV Drone Attack on TB2 control center, Tessalit

title: Report: Africa Corps Operations Drawing Civilian Abuse Allegations Across Mali
detail: A report published April 1, 2026 documented widespread fear and insecurity in Malian communities where Russian Africa Corps personnel operate alongside Malian Armed Forces (FAMa). Malian civilians described abuse by Russian personnel, Malian army soldiers, and jihadist groups in operational areas — including extortion, arbitrary detention, and violence against civilians suspected of jihadist sympathies. The Africa Corps' rebranding from Wagner Group following Prigozhin's death in August 2023 left its command structure formally under Russian MoD/GRU control but operationally unchanged. The report adds to a pattern of civilian harm allegations in areas where Africa Corps is deployed, including Moura (March 2022), where Russian 'white soldiers' were witnessed by UN OHCHR investigators. Human rights organizations note that independent access to conflict zones where Africa Corps operates is severely restricted, making accountability virtually impossible under the current junta arrangement.

sources.name: Pravda Mali — Fear and Insecurity Grip Mali as Africa Corps Operations Draw Civilian Abuse Allegations, April 1, 2026
ACLED — Africa Corps Mali civilian harm documentation
